mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near zero, further declines in policy rates indicate renewed easing and lower discount rates, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days should be positive. The signal is falsified if rate cuts at this level do not coincide with positive forward equity performance.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines from an already low level and 20-day SPX returns are negative in most triggered months, or the hit rate is not above 55% in rolling 3-year windows, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals deteriorating household income and weaker earnings expectations, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days should be negative once the unemployment rate crosses the trigger. The effect is confirmed if equity weakness follows labor-market stress consistently.
failure_condition: If UNRATE trigger events are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or the average forward return is not below unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point, reject it.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broad consumer demand supported by payroll growth, which should lift near-term corporate revenue expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if sales growth occurs without market appreciation.
failure_condition: If joint high-growth observations in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not outperform single-factor cases, or triggered instances do not deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of samples, reject the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO signals improving industrial output and capacity utilization, which typically tightens earnings risk premiums and supports equity prices over the next 60 trading days. The hypothesis is falsified if industrial upswings are not followed by positive medium-horizon stock returns.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods where INDPRO is rising on a 3-month basis, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation stays elevated while FEDFUNDS remains unusually low, real policy rates are deeply negative and liquidity conditions are accommodative, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails if low real-rate conditions do not produce an immediate risk-on response.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rising for 3 straight months while FEDFUNDS < 1%
failure_condition: If episodes with rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS below 1% do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or the mean return is not above baseline, reject the mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after prior strength signals housing demand weakening before broader consumption slows, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should turn negative. The mechanism is confirmed if housing rollovers systematically precede equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the average 60-day return is not materially below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor market, rising PAYEMS together with falling UNRATE indicates resilient hiring and improving income flows, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if labor improvement does not translate into equity strength.
failure_condition: If instances with rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fail to outperform all other labor states, or 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 60% of cases, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, a rise in FEDFUNDS combined with sticky CPIAUCSL indicates policy is becoming more restrictive in real terms, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The claim fails if tightening does not coincide with negative equity reactions.
failure_condition: Reject if tightening episodes with nondecelerating CPIAUCSL are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of samples, or if the average return is not below zero.
